title: arctostaphylos parryana subsp. parryana
intro: Arctostaphylos parryana subsp. parryana, also known as Parry's manzanita, is a shrub plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a type of manzanita, a genus of plants that are part of the heath family (Ericaceae), and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and berries have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including colds, coughs, and digestive problems, and have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Herbal preparations of Parry's manzanita include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made from the leaves and berries, and are often used to promote overall health and well-being.
